extraction.one_line_summary: HAYTER Ethan won a reduced sprint after INEOS paced hard on late drags, with SHEFFIELD Magnus delivering a massive leadout from distance.
tactical_patterns: INEOS Grenadiers (DE PLUS) set infernal tempo on uncategorized drags pre-20km, eliminating pure sprinters like VERNON before organized leadouts formed.
SHEFFIELD Magnus executed abnormally long leadout in reduced field sprint, suggesting INEOS prioritized positioning over saving energy in hilly sprint stages.
UAE Emirates (POLANC) shared pacemaking despite no sprinter, revealing willingness to control for GC positioning even on non-mountain stages.
VLASOV Alexandr best-placed GC rider in sprint finish, indicating BORA strategy to gain time bonuses rather than conserve energy.

rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: First WorldTour road win after previous disappointment suggests form building through stage race progression
rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: negative: Dropped on long drags with 20km remaining reveals insufficient climbing tolerance for hilly sprint stages
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: positive: Delivering massive leadouts in reduced sprints demonstrates exceptional power ceiling beyond typical domestique role
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexandr: positive: Finishing fourth in chaotic sprint shows sharp positioning skills and willingness to contest bonifications actively
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: JUMBO controlled all day without exhausting leader, indicating comfort defending jersey on semi-hilly terrain
